title: commiphora serrata
intro: Commiphora serrata, also known as Khaya or Indian tree, is a deciduous tree native to India and other parts of Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral activities, making it useful in treating various health conditions. Some of the health benefits associated with Commiphora serrata include reduced inflammation, improved immune function, and potential antitumor activities. The herbal preparations of Commiphora serrata typically involve the bark or resin, which can be used to make teas, tinctures, or capsules, often used in traditional Ayurvedic medicine.
